description:
You are the system administrator for a small internal tool called “myapp”.
    The project lives in the directory:

        /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/

    Right now it contains two text files that matter for this task:

        1. /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/VERSION
        2. /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/CHANGELOG.md

    Current situation
    -----------------
    • The file “VERSION” contains just one line, the string “2.3.4”.
    • At the very top of “CHANGELOG.md” you can see the most-recent entry:

            ## [2.3.4] - 2023-09-15
            ### Added
             - Initial deployment of system-wide SELinux policies.

      (Older historic entries follow underneath but they are irrelevant to this task.)

    Goal
    ----
    1. Bump myapp to the next patch level, i.e. turn version 2.3.4 into 2.3.5
       strictly following semantic-versioning rules (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).
    2. Record that bump in the changelog **as the new first section** with the exact
       text shown below.
    3. Produce a very small human-readable log file named 
       “/home/user/infra-tools/myapp/version_bump.log” that captures the change.

    Detailed requirements
    ---------------------
    A. VERSION file
       • After you are done the entire content of /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/VERSION
         must be exactly one line:

             2.3.5

    B. CHANGELOG.md
       • You must insert the following block *at the very top* of the file
         directly above the current “## [2.3.4] …” section:

             ## [2.3.5] - 2023-10-31
             ### Fixed
              - Applied latest security patches to systemd service files.

       • Do not change anything else inside CHANGELOG.md.
       • Make sure there is a single blank line between this new entry and the
         previous “## [2.3.4] …” section (as is customary in Markdown).

    C. version_bump.log
       • Create a brand-new file /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/version_bump.log
         containing exactly three lines:

             Old version: 2.3.4
             New version: 2.3.5
             Changelog entry added successfully.

         (No extra spaces at line ends, newline at EOF is fine.)

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Perform the task using typical shell utilities (echo, sed, printf, etc.).
    • No sudo/root access is available.
    • Only touch the three files mentioned above; nothing else should be altered
      or created.

    Verification notes (what the automated checker will test)
    ----------------------------------------------------------
    1. VERSION contains the single line “2.3.5”.
    2. CHANGELOG.md starts with the four-line block shown in section B and is
       followed by a blank line and then the existing “## [2.3.4] …” header.
    3. version_bump.log exists with the exact three lines listed in section C.

    Once finished, you can display the contents of the three files to confirm
    your work, but that is optional.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

      /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/          (directory, 0755)
      /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/VERSION   (file, 0644)
        └─ content: "2.3.4\n"

      /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/CHANGELOG.md (file, 0644)
        └─ content:
           "## [2.3.4] - 2023-09-15\n"
           "### Added\n"
           " - Initial deployment of system-wide SELinux policies.\n"
           "\n"
           "## [2.2.0] - 2023-08-02\n"
           "### Changed\n"
           " - Upgraded nginx to 1.22.1\n"
           "\n"
           "…(older history continues)…\n"

    Expected final filesystem state after successful completion:

      /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/VERSION
        └─ content: "2.3.5\n"

      /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/CHANGELOG.md
        └─ content (first 9 lines only):
           "## [2.3.5] - 2023-10-31\n"
           "### Fixed\n"
           " - Applied latest security patches to systemd service files.\n"
           "\n"
           "## [2.3.4] - 2023-09-15\n"
           "### Added\n"
           " - Initial deployment of system-wide SELinux policies.\n"
           "\n"

      /home/user/infra-tools/myapp/version_bump.log
        └─ content:
           "Old version: 2.3.4\n"
           "New version: 2.3.5\n"
           "Changelog entry added successfully.\n"
